languageModelTools.name: list_scratches
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Lists all scratch files in the user's scratch storage, optionally filtered.
Use when user asks to see available scratches or find files by pattern or directory.

Parameters:
- filter (optional): A glob pattern, a bare basename, a path prefix, or a scratch:/// URI.
  - Glob pattern: any string containing *, ?, {, or [ (e.g. "**/*.ts", "projects/foo/**", "*.md")
    Glob patterns that do not already start with "**/" are automatically prepended with "**/",
    so all globs match recursively across the full tree (e.g. "*.md" matches all .md files, not just root-level).
  - Bare basename: a single name with no "/" (e.g. "README.md", "testing") is expanded to "**/name"
    and matches files with that name at any depth.
  - Path prefix: a slash-separated relative path (e.g. "projects/foo/", "projects/foo") matches all
    scratch paths that start with it. Trailing slash is optional.
  - scratch:/// URI: scheme is stripped and the appropriate rule above is applied.
  All forms match against the root-relative path without a leading slash.

Examples:
- {} → lists all scratches
- { filter: "projects/my-app/" } → all scratches under projects/my-app/
- { filter: "projects/my-app" } → same as above, trailing slash is optional
- { filter: "scratch:///projects/my-app" } → same, using scratch:/// URI
- { filter: "**/*.ts" } → all TypeScript files recursively
- { filter: "*.ts" } → same as **/*.ts, auto-expanded to match recursively
- { filter: "README.md" } → all files named README.md anywhere in the tree
- { filter: "projects/foo/**" } → all files under projects/foo/ via glob

Returns: Newline-separated paths (without leading slash) or empty string if none match.

filter.description: Filter to use when listing scratches. Accepts a glob pattern (e.g. "*.md", "projects/**"), a bare basename with no "/" (e.g. "README.md" — matched recursively at any depth), a path prefix (e.g. "projects/foo"), or a scratch:/// URI. Glob patterns and bare basenames without a leading **/ are automatically matched recursively. If omitted, all scratches are listed. Only use if a specific directory or set of files is needed, otherwise prefer leaving it empty.

languageModelTools.name: read_scratch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Reads content from a scratch file
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Reads the content of a scratch file, optionally reading a specific line range.
Use when user asks to view or retrieve scratch content.

Parameters:
- uri (required): Scratch URI (e.g., "scratch:///notes.md") or URI string
- lineFrom (optional): Starting line number (0-based, inclusive)
- lineTo (optional): Ending line number (0-based, exclusive)

Examples:
- { uri: "scratch:///notes.md" } → full file content
- { uri: "scratch:///code.ts", lineFrom: 10, lineTo: 20 } → lines 10-19

Returns: File content as string.

uri.description: The scratch URI to read from. Must be a valid scratch:// URI or URI string.

lineFrom.description: Optional starting line number (0-based, inclusive). If omitted, reads from the beginning.

lineTo.description: Optional ending line number (0-based, exclusive). If omitted, reads to the end.

languageModelTools.name: write_scratch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Writes content to a scratch file
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Writes content to a scratch file, creating it if it doesn't exist or overwriting if it does.
Use when user asks to save, create, or update scratch content.

Parameters:
- uri (required): Scratch URI (e.g., "scratch:///notes.md") or URI string
- content (required): Content to write to the file

Examples:
- { uri: "scratch:///notes.md", content: "My notes" } → creates/overwrites file
- { uri: "scratch:///code/test.ts", content: "const x = 1;" } → creates nested file

Returns: "Scratch written successfully.". After writing always provide a short (1-2 lines) sumary of changes made,

uri.description: The scratch URI to write to. Must be a valid scratch:// URI or URI string. Parent directories will be created if needed.

content.description: The content to write to the file. Will overwrite existing content if file exists.

languageModelTools.name: rename_scratch
languageModelTools.userDescription: Renames or moves a scratch file
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Renames or moves a scratch file from one URI to another.
Use when user asks to rename or move a scratch file to a new location or name.

NOTE: This tool modifies the file system and cannot be undone. Always confirm the old and new URIs with the user before proceeding.

Parameters:
- oldUri (required): Current scratch URI (e.g., "scratch:///notes.md") or URI string
- newUri (required): New scratch URI (e.g., "scratch:///renamed-notes.md") or URI string

Examples:
- { oldUri: "scratch:///notes.md", newUri: "scratch:///notes-backup.md" } → renames file
- { oldUri: "scratch:///misc/todo.txt", newUri: "scratch:///lists/todo.txt" } → moves file

Returns: "Scratch renamed successfully."

oldUri.description: The current scratch URI to rename from. Must be a valid scratch:// URI or URI string.

newUri.description: The new scratch URI to rename to. Must be a valid scratch:// URI or URI string. Parent directories will be created if needed.

languageModelTools.name: search_scratches
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Searches across scratch file contents using text or regex patterns, returning matches with context lines.
Use when user asks to find specific content, patterns, or references across scratches.

Parameters:
- query (required): Search term or regex pattern
- isRegex (optional): Treat query as regex (default: false)
- caseSensitive (optional): Case-sensitive search (default: false)
- filter (optional): Glob pattern, bare basename, path prefix, or scratch:/// URI to limit scope.
  - Glob (e.g. "**/*.md", "*.md", "projects/**"): patterns without a leading "**/" are prepended with it and match recursively.
  - Bare basename (no "/", e.g. "README.md"): expanded to "**/name" and matches files anywhere in the tree.
  - Path prefix (contains "/", e.g. "projects/foo"): restricts search to that directory subtree.
  - scratch:/// URI: scheme is stripped and the appropriate rule above is applied.
- contextLines (optional): Lines before/after to include (default: 2)
- maxResults (optional): Maximum matches to return (default: 100)

Examples:
- { query: "authentication" } → finds all occurrences with 2 lines context
- { query: "TODO|FIXME", isRegex: true } → finds TODOs using regex
- { query: "function", filter: "projects/my-app" } → finds in all files under projects/my-app/
- { query: "function", filter: "scratch:///projects/my-app" } → same, using scratch:/// URI
- { query: "function", filter: "**/*.ts", contextLines: 0 } → finds in TypeScript files, no context

Returns: formatted text listing each match as "path:line → content" with surrounding context lines indented by two spaces.

query.description: The search term or regex pattern. Required.

isRegex.description: Whether to treat the query as a regex pattern. Default: false.

caseSensitive.description: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Default: false.

filter.description: Glob pattern (e.g. "**/*.md", "*.md"), bare basename (e.g. "README.md" — matched anywhere in the tree), path prefix (e.g. "projects/foo" — restricts to that directory), or scratch:/// URI to limit search scope. Glob patterns and bare basenames without a leading **/ are automatically matched recursively.

contextLines.description: Number of context lines to include before and after each match. Default: 2.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Default: 100.

scratches.scratchDirectory.description: Directory where scratch files are stored, defaults to extension's global storage directory

viewsWelcome.contents:
No scratches found
[Create a new one](command:scratches.newScratch)

scripts.test:ui: npm run compile && npx extest run-tests 'out/ui-test/**/*.test.js' --storage .uitest-temp/ --code_version max --type stable --extensions_dir .uitest-temp/extensions
scripts.test: npm run test:unit && npm run test:ui
